revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Top level categories]] [[Category:Top level categories]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Top level categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Dd]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipediawikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी

When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[category:Fertility goddesses]] [[category:Japanese goddesses]] [[category:Beauty goddesses]] [[category:Fortune goddesses]] [[category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipediawikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[category:Fertility goddesses]] [[category:Japanese goddesses]] [[category:Beauty goddesses]] [[category:Fortune goddesses]] [[category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and categories from WikipediaWikipedia]] [[Category:ddk]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:ddk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[category:Fertility goddesses]] [[category:Japanese goddesses]] [[category:Beauty goddesses]] [[category:Fortune goddesses]] [[category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Kisshōten]]]] [[category:ddk]] [[category:kk]] [[category:attempting to force efresh]] [[category:ファイル]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kisshōten]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:1 revision imported]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[category:Fertility goddesses]] [[category:Japanese goddesses]] [[category:Beauty goddesses]] [[category:Fortune goddesses]] [[category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Kisshōten]]]] [[category:ddk]] [[category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kisshōten]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[category:Fertility goddesses]] [[category:Japanese goddesses]] [[category:Beauty goddesses]] [[category:Fortune goddesses]] [[category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Kisshōten]]]] [[category:ddk]] [[category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kisshōten]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kisshoten}} [[Category:Buddhist goddesses]] [[Category:Fertility goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Beauty goddesses]] [[Category:Fortune goddesses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Kisshōten]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Kisshōten]]]] [[Category:ddk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kisshōten]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

"Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] to [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Goddess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Goddess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category inline|Kichijouten}}
