revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{citation needed|Kisshoten}} ]] {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] [[Category:Fertility go11esses]] [[Category:Japanese go11esses]] [[Category:Beauty go11esses]] [[Category:Fortune go11esses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11 6 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kisshōten]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:28 legions]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]]

Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{citation needed|Kisshoten}} ]] {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] ([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{citation needed|Kisshoten}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kisshōten]] [[Category:28 legions]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]]

revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{citation needed|Kisshoten}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] [[Category:Fertility go11esses]] [[Category:Japanese go11esses]] [[Category:Beauty go11esses]] [[Category:Fortune go11esses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kisshōten]] [[Category:28 legions]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]]

revision.comment: Bot: mark page as missing translated-page template
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] [[Category:Fertility go11esses]] [[Category:Japanese go11esses]] [[Category:Beauty go11esses]] [[Category:Fortune go11esses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Kisshōten]] [[Category:28 legions]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Kisshōten]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist go11esses]] [[Category:Japanese go11esses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:28 legions]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Buddhist go11esses
revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese go11esses]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[Hinduism in Japan]] * [[Kagome crest]] * [[Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category revision.text:
{{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[Satkona]] * [[Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

"Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] go11ess [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the go11ess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-go11ess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] go11esses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing
