revision.comment: Text replacement - "go11ess" to "goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sn:Kisshōten]] [[en:Kisshōten]] [[sn:isshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[en:Kisshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[en:Kisshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]] of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddess[[Category:replace 11 with dd]]es are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] }} [[en:Kisshōten]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}} [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] ([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
([[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]]: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
([[:en:Revised Romanization of Korean|RR]]: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]], adapted via [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]] from the [[:en:Hinduism|Hindu]] goddess [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]]. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''[[:en:Kagome crest|kagome]]''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu [[:en:Tridevi|Tridevi]] goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * [[:en:Hinduism in Japan|Hinduism in Japan]] * [[:en:Kagome crest|Kagome crest]] * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** [[:en:Hexagram|Hexagram]] ** [[:en:Octagram|Octagram]] ** [[:en:Hexagon|Hexagon]] ** [[:en:Octagon|Octagon]] * [[:en:Kisshō Tennyo|''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)]] * [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Star of Lakshmi|Star of Lakshmi]] ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * [[:en:Vasudhara|Vasudhara]] (''[[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]]'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], [[:en:Wealth|Wealth]], [[:en:Fertility|Fertility]], [[:en:Prosperity|Prosperity]]) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} [[en:Kisshōten]] {{Commons category|Kichijouten}} [[fa:کیشوتن]] [[fr:Kichijōten]] [[it:Kisshōten]] [[ja:吉祥天]] [[ko:길상천]] [[pl:Kichijōten]] [[sv:Kisshōten]] [[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]] [[th:คิชโชเต็ง]] [[vi:Kichijōten]] {{wikidata link|Q2646981}} [[Category:Forms of Lakshmi]] [[Category:Japanese Vajrayana Buddhism]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:replace 11 with dd]] [[Category:Twenty-Four Protective Deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity}}, adapted via {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism}} from the {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hindu}} goddess {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}}. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''{{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=kagome}}''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu {{ill|Tridevi|en|Tridevi|WD=Q1137755|lt=Tridevi}} goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing {{ill|Parvati|en|Parvati|WD=Q170485|lt=Parvati}} and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * {{ill|Hinduism in Japan|en|Hinduism in Japan|WD=Q17049486|lt=Hinduism in Japan}} * {{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=Kagome crest}} * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** {{ill|Hexagram|en|Hexagram|WD=Q1164668|lt=Hexagram}} ** {{ill|Octagram|en|Octagram|WD=Q2622857|lt=Octagram}} ** {{ill|Hexagon|en|Hexagon|WD=Q83043|lt=Hexagon}} ** {{ill|Octagon|en|Octagon|WD=Q166080|lt=Octagon}} * {{ill|Kisshō Tennyo|en|Kisshō Tennyo|WD=Q6417193|lt=''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)}} * {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} ** {{ill|Star of Lakshmi|en|Star of Lakshmi|WD=Q4524287|lt=Star of Lakshmi}} ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * {{ill|Vasudhara|en|Vasudhara|WD=Q3536166|lt=Vasudhara}} (''{{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}}'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], {{ill|Wealth|en|Wealth|WD=Q200535|lt=Wealth}}, {{ill|Fertility|en|Fertility|WD=Q964401|lt=Fertility}}, {{ill|Prosperity|en|Prosperity|WD=Q1760011|lt=Prosperity}}) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2646981]] Wikidata name: Kisshōten [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
({{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|WD=Q42222|lt=Pinyin}}: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
({{ill|Revised Romanization of Korean|en|Revised Romanization of Korean|WD=Q498721|lt=RR}}: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity}}, adapted via {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism}} from the {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hindu}} goddess {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}}. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''{{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=kagome}}''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu {{ill|Tridevi|en|Tridevi|WD=Q1137755|lt=Tridevi}} goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing {{ill|Parvati|en|Parvati|WD=Q170485|lt=Parvati}} and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * {{ill|Hinduism in Japan|en|Hinduism in Japan|WD=Q17049486|lt=Hinduism in Japan}} * {{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=Kagome crest}} * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** {{ill|Hexagram|en|Hexagram|WD=Q1164668|lt=Hexagram}} ** {{ill|Octagram|en|Octagram|WD=Q2622857|lt=Octagram}} ** {{ill|Hexagon|en|Hexagon|WD=Q83043|lt=Hexagon}} ** {{ill|Octagon|en|Octagon|WD=Q166080|lt=Octagon}} * {{ill|Kisshō Tennyo|en|Kisshō Tennyo|WD=Q6417193|lt=''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)}} * {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} ** {{ill|Star of Lakshmi|en|Star of Lakshmi|WD=Q4524287|lt=Star of Lakshmi}} ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * {{ill|Vasudhara|en|Vasudhara|WD=Q3536166|lt=Vasudhara}} (''{{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}}'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], {{ill|Wealth|en|Wealth|WD=Q200535|lt=Wealth}}, {{ill|Fertility|en|Fertility|WD=Q964401|lt=Fertility}}, {{ill|Prosperity|en|Prosperity|WD=Q1760011|lt=Prosperity}}) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity}}, adapted via {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism}} from the {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hindu}} goddess {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}}. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''{{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=kagome}}''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu {{ill|Tridevi|en|Tridevi|WD=Q1137755|lt=Tridevi}} goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing {{ill|Parvati|en|Parvati|WD=Q170485|lt=Parvati}} and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * {{ill|Hinduism in Japan|en|Hinduism in Japan|WD=Q17049486|lt=Hinduism in Japan}} * {{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=Kagome crest}} * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** {{ill|Hexagram|en|Hexagram|WD=Q1164668|lt=Hexagram}} ** {{ill|Octagram|en|Octagram|WD=Q2622857|lt=Octagram}} ** {{ill|Hexagon|en|Hexagon|WD=Q83043|lt=Hexagon}} ** {{ill|Octagon|en|Octagon|WD=Q166080|lt=Octagon}} * {{ill|Kisshō Tennyo|en|Kisshō Tennyo|WD=Q6417193|lt=''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)}} * {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} ** {{ill|Star of Lakshmi|en|Star of Lakshmi|WD=Q4524287|lt=Star of Lakshmi}} ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * {{ill|Vasudhara|en|Vasudhara|WD=Q3536166|lt=Vasudhara}} (''{{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}}'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], {{ill|Wealth|en|Wealth|WD=Q200535|lt=Wealth}}, {{ill|Fertility|en|Fertility|WD=Q964401|lt=Fertility}}, {{ill|Prosperity|en|Prosperity|WD=Q1760011|lt=Prosperity}}) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External [[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[co:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kichijouten]][[en:Śrīmahādevī]][[fa:کیشوتن]][[fr:Kichijōten]][[it:Kisshōten]][[ja:吉祥天]][[ko:길상천]][[pl:Kichijōten]][[simple:Kisshōten]][[sv:Kisshōten]][[ta:கிச்சிசோடென்]][[th:คิชโชเต็ง]][[vi:Kichijōten]] {{short description|Japanese female deity}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox Buddha | name = Śrīmahādevī (Buddhism) | image = Jyoruriji_Kissyoten_Srii.jpg |image caption = Statue of Kisshoutennyo holding her [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]], from the [[Jōruri-ji|Jyoruri temple]] in [[Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa]] | sanskrit_name = श्रीमहादेवी
Śrīmahādevī | burmese_name = | chinese_name = 吉祥天
({{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|WD=Q42222|lt=Pinyin|1=Pinyin}}: ''Jíxiáng tiān'') | japanese_name = {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きっしょうてん}} or {{ruby-ja|吉祥天|きちじょうてん}}
([[:en:Romaji|Romaji]]: ''Kichijōten'') | karen_name = | khmer_name = | korean_name = 길상천
({{ill|Revised Romanization of Korean|en|Revised Romanization of Korean|WD=Q498721|lt=RR|1=Revised Romanization of Korean}}: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity|1=Deva (Buddhism)}}, adapted via {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism in Japan}} from the {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hindu|1=Hinduism}} goddess {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}}. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''{{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=kagome|1=Kagome crest}}''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu {{ill|Tridevi|en|Tridevi|WD=Q1137755|lt=Tridevi|1=Tridevi}} goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing {{ill|Parvati|en|Parvati|WD=Q170485|lt=Parvati|1=Parvati}} and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * {{ill|Hinduism in Japan|en|Hinduism in Japan|WD=Q17049486|lt=Hinduism in Japan|1=Hinduism in Japan}} * {{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=Kagome crest|1=Kagome crest}} * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** {{ill|Hexagram|en|Hexagram|WD=Q1164668|lt=Hexagram|1=Hexagram}} ** {{ill|Octagram|en|Octagram|WD=Q2622857|lt=Octagram|1=Octagram}} ** {{ill|Hexagon|en|Hexagon|WD=Q83043|lt=Hexagon|1=Hexagon}} ** {{ill|Octagon|en|Octagon|WD=Q166080|lt=Octagon|1=Octagon}} * {{ill|Kisshō Tennyo|en|Kisshō Tennyo|WD=Q6417193|lt=''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)|1=Kisshō Tennyo}} * {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}} ** {{ill|Star of Lakshmi|en|Star of Lakshmi|WD=Q4524287|lt=Star of Lakshmi|1=Star of Lakshmi}} ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * {{ill|Vasudhara|en|Vasudhara|WD=Q3536166|lt=Vasudhara|1=Vasudhara}} (''{{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}}'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], {{ill|Wealth|en|Wealth|WD=Q200535|lt=Wealth|1=Wealth}}, {{ill|Fertility|en|Fertility|WD=Q964401|lt=Fertility|1=Fertility}}, {{ill|Prosperity|en|Prosperity|WD=Q1760011|lt=Prosperity|1=Prosperity}}) ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category inline|Kichijouten}} {{Japanese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} ({{ill|Revised Romanization of Korean|en|Revised Romanization of Korean|WD=Q498721|lt=RR|1=Revised Romanization of Korean}}: ''Gilsang Cheon'') | mongolian_name = | okinawan_name = | shan_name = | tagalog_name = Slimahadevi | thai_name = | tibetan_name = ལྷ་མོ་ཆེན་མོ་དཔལ།་
Wylie: lha mo chen mo dpal | vietnamese_name = Cát Tường Thiên | sinhalese_name = | veneration = [[:en:Mahāyāna|Mahāyāna]], [[:en:Vajrayāna|Vajrayāna]] }} '''Kisshōten''' ({{lang|ja|[[Wiktionary:吉祥天|吉祥天]]}}, lit. "Auspicious Heavens"), also known as '''Kichijōten''', '''Kisshoutennyo''' (吉祥天女), or '''Kudokuten''' (功徳天), is a Japanese female {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity|1=Deva (Buddhism)}}, adapted via {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism in Japan}} from the {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hindu|1=Hinduism}} goddess {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}}. Kisshoutennyo is sometimes named as one of the [[:en:Seven Gods of Fortune|Seven Gods of Fortune]] (''fukujin''), replacing either [[Jurōjin]] or [[Fukurokuju]].{{cite web|title=Wooden figure of Kichijōten|url=https://www.britishmuseum.org/explore/highlights/highlight_objects/asia/w/wooden_figure_of_kichij%C5%8Dten.aspx|publisher=The British Museum|access-date=27 August 2012}} For example, in the 1783 edition of the [[Butsuzōzui|Butsuzōzui compendium]] (reprinted in 1796), Kichijōten replaces [[Fukurokuju]] as one of the seven fukujin.{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(077.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} She is considered to be the goddess of happiness, fertility, and beauty.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title=Kichijoten Japanese Lucky Go11ess of Beauty |url=https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |access-date=2023-03-01 |archive-date=10 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230210030230/https://mythologysource.com/kichijotenl-japanese-goddess/ |url-status=bot: unknown }}{{cite web|title=Kisshōten (Kichijōten)|url=http://www.philamuseum.org/collections/permanent/305645.html|publisher=Philadelphia Museum of Art|access-date=27 August 2012}} Kisshoutennyo's iconography is distinguished by the [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju gem (如意宝珠)]] in her hand,{{cite web |last1=Neighbour-Parent |first1=Mary |title=Kichijouten 吉祥天 |url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kichijouten.htm |website=JAANUS |publisher=Atsumi International Scholarship Foundation}} Kisshōten and the Nyoihōju gem are both represented by the symbol of the ''{{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=kagome|1=Kagome crest}}''. When Kisshoutennyo is counted among the seven fukujin and fellow Fukujin [[Daikokuten#Go11ess Daikokutennyo|Daikoku]] is regarded in feminine form,{{cite web|title=Butsuzōzui (Illustrated Compendium of Buddhist Images)|url=http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|location=Ehime University Library|page=(059.jpg)|language=ja|format=digital photos|date=1796|access-date=14 May 2016|archive-date=10 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181010061804/http://www.lib.ehime-u.ac.jp/SUZUKA/316/index.html|url-status=dead}} all three of the Hindu {{ill|Tridevi|en|Tridevi|WD=Q1137755|lt=Tridevi|1=Tridevi}} goddesses are represented in the Fukujin, with Daikoku representing {{ill|Parvati|en|Parvati|WD=Q170485|lt=Parvati|1=Parvati}} and [[Benzaiten]] representing [[Saraswati]]. ==See also== * [[Japanese Buddhist pantheon#Level 4: Heavenly deities .28Ten-bu.29|Buddhist Tenbu (天部) deities]] * [[Seven Lucky Gods|Fukujin (福神) deities]] * {{ill|Hinduism in Japan|en|Hinduism in Japan|WD=Q17049486|lt=Hinduism in Japan|1=Hinduism in Japan}} * {{ill|Kagome crest|en|Kagome crest|WD=Q16851345|lt=Kagome crest|1=Kagome crest}} * [[:en:Kagome lattice|Kagome lattice]] ** {{ill|Hexagram|en|Hexagram|WD=Q1164668|lt=Hexagram|1=Hexagram}} ** {{ill|Octagram|en|Octagram|WD=Q2622857|lt=Octagram|1=Octagram}} ** {{ill|Hexagon|en|Hexagon|WD=Q83043|lt=Hexagon|1=Hexagon}} ** {{ill|Octagon|en|Octagon|WD=Q166080|lt=Octagon|1=Octagon}} * {{ill|Kisshō Tennyo|en|Kisshō Tennyo|WD=Q6417193|lt=''Kisshō Tennyo'' (manga)|1=Kisshō Tennyo}} * {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}} ** {{ill|Star of Lakshmi|en|Star of Lakshmi|WD=Q4524287|lt=Star of Lakshmi|1=Star of Lakshmi}} ** [[:en:Ashtalakshmi|Ashtalakshmi]] * [[Cintamani#Nomenclature.2C orthography and etymology|Nyoihōju (如意宝珠) wishing gem]] * [[:en:Satkona|Satkona]] * {{ill|Vasudhara|en|Vasudhara|WD=Q3536166|lt=Vasudhara|1=Vasudhara}} (''{{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}}'' of [[:en:Good fortune|Good fortune]], {{ill|Wealth|en|Wealth|WD=Q200535|lt=Wealth|1=Wealth}}, {{ill|Fertility|en|Fertility|WD=Q964401|lt=Fertility|1=Fertility}}, {{ill|Prosperity|en|Prosperity|WD=Q1760011|lt=Prosperity|1=Prosperity}})
